summary: Bug fix verification PASSED: PDF annotation labels for wall measurements now show calculated m² value instead of raw jm. Testing confirmed labels match TakeoffPanel values.
critical_code_review_comments: Bug fix verified in MeasurementOverlay.js lines 215-221: Wall type measurements now calculate effectiveM2 = (quantity × wallHeight × bothSidesFactor) - openings for PDF label display
Calculation logic matches TakeoffPanel.calculateRow() ensuring consistent values between PDF annotation and panel
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix for PDF annotation labels showing wrong values is verified. Wall measurements now display calculated m² on both PDF canvas and TakeoffPanel, matching exactly.

tests_executed.test: Create wall measurement and verify PDF annotation shows calculated m²
tests_executed.details: Created wall (Huoltomaalaus) with default height 2.6m. PDF annotation showed '16.08 m²' matching panel value. Raw jm was ~6.19, calculated m² = 6.19 × 2.6 = 16.09 m²

tests_executed.test: Rectangle/polygon measurements show correct m² values
tests_executed.details: Created rectangle (Kipsikatto). PDF annotation showed '6.18 m²' matching panel value exactly. Rectangle area calculation working correctly.

tests_executed.test: Edit wall measurement height and openings - verify PDF annotation updates
tests_executed.details: Changed wall height from 2.6m to 3.5m and added 2.5 m² openings. PDF annotation and panel both updated to show new calculated value.

tests_executed.test: PDF annotation label matches TakeoffPanel value
tests_executed.details: All created measurements (wall and rectangle) showed identical values on PDF canvas annotation and in right-side TakeoffPanel. Summary totals also matched.

bug_fix_verification.original_issue: PDF annotation labels showed raw quantity (jm) for wall measurements instead of calculated m²
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: MeasurementOverlay.drawMeasurement() was displaying measurement.quantity directly for wall types instead of calculating effectiveM2
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: Lines 215-221 in MeasurementOverlay.js now check for wall type and calculate: bruttoM2 = quantity × wallHeight × bothSidesFactor, then effectiveM2 = bruttoM2 - openings
bug_fix_verification.verification_result: PASSED - PDF annotations now show calculated m² matching TakeoffPanel values
tested_scenarios: Value consistency between PDF annotation and TakeoffPanel

MeasurementOverlay.js.wall_label_fix: Lines 215-221: Correctly calculates effectiveM2 for wall type before displaying label
